directive:
Continue angle 0 with a wholesale mathematical rewrite that preserves the field-tier kernel—union-honest adaptive expected-length inference for the binary optimal-policy value—but removes every undefined or overclaimed component. Define a full-data law F for (X,A,Y(0),Y(1)), impose consistency and conditional exchangeability on F, define the observation map O=(X,A,Y(A)), and define each observed-data stratum P_j as the image of an explicitly specified full-data class F_j. State a common dominating measure and measurable versions of e_P and m_{P,a}. Restrict each zeta_j to an explicit admissible set, including 0 <= p_{j,-} <= p_{j,+} <= 1, compatibility of the margin, Hölder, overlap, design-density, and outcome-noise conditions, and prove nonemptiness by exhibiting at least one member of every declared stratum; delete any empty or incompatible stratum.

Redefine the confidence-interval decision class to contain only sample-measurable, almost surely finite, connected closed intervals [L_n,U_n] with L_n <= U_n and length U_n-L_n. Decide explicitly between finite-sample honesty and uniform asymptotic honesty. If finite-sample coverage is claimed, provide observable concentration bounds or honest nuisance confidence sets that yield it; nuisance L2 rates alone do not justify finite-sample coverage. If uniform asymptotic honesty is intended, replace the current exact-for-every-n definition and theorem quantifiers by a precise liminf_{n->infinity} inf_{P in U} coverage statement and formulate the expected-length frontier on the same asymptotic scale.

Replace the prose “cross-fitted smoothing handle” by a complete finite-sample formula. Fix the number and allocation of folds; define a deterministic finite scale grid H_n; specify the smoothing map, for example phi_h(u0,u1)=h log(exp(u0/h)+exp(u1/h)), its derivative weights pi_{h,a}, and the foldwise orthogonal score
psi_h(O;eta)=phi_h(m_0(X),m_1(X))+sum_{a=0}^1 1{A=a} pi_{h,a}(m_0(X),m_1(X)) (Y-m_a(X))/e_a(X).
Define the cross-fitted average, every truncation used to preserve overlap and boundedness, the empirical variance and variance floor, the studentizer, a mathematically explicit uniform smoothing-plus-nuisance bias envelope b_j(h,n), its union envelope, the exact finite-grid scale-selection/Lepski rule including tie breaking, and the final connected bias-aware interval endpoints. State which quantities are known, estimated, or calibrated and make the entire output a measurable function of O_1,...,O_n.

Add law-primitive, uniform nuisance-estimability assumptions that are sufficient for each displayed remainder bound and are matched by named construction-side learners. At minimum specify the design density, smoothness/radius of both outcome regressions and the propensity (or a known/randomized propensity), overlap, conditional variance bounds needed for studentization and lower bounds, and uniform foldwise probability bounds for the relevant L2 and, where used, sup-norm errors and their products relative to every h in H_n. Give an actual learner—such as clipped local-polynomial, wavelet, or series regression—with tuning rules that attains the asserted rates uniformly. Do not assume that an estimator already has the desired value-functional error, coverage, bias envelope, or modulus rate: that would encode the conclusion and launder the theorem.

Retain the adaptive frontier as a theorem-to-prove in D0 only after stating a precise theorem whose construction and assumptions are sufficient to make uniform honesty and the upper length bound genuinely derivable. Do not demote it merely because it is difficult or currently unproved. However, if the exact construction cannot be shown to attain the ordered modulus under the declared classes, reclassify the two-sided frontier honestly as a conjecture; retain as theorem targets only (i) the testing/ordered-modulus lower bound with the correct product-Hellinger constants and (ii) a separate uniform-validity and explicit upper-length theorem for the constructed interval, without claiming that its upper rate equals omega^+.

The revision must replace the abstract “asymp omega” headline by an explicit adaptation diagram. Supply least-favorable full-data submodels, verify that their observed images remain in the relevant strata, calculate their Hellinger distances and value separations, and state matching upper and lower rates—including constants or logarithmic losses—for at least: (1) a uniformly separated, no-tie regime yielding the root-n rate; and (2) a genuinely nonregular near-tie or positive-tie regime whose rate is derived as an explicit function of d, s, alpha, and the tie-mass bounds. Also identify and prove the boundary between pairs of strata for which one honest interval can attain both stratumwise rates and pairs for which the between-class modulus forces an adaptation penalty. If the second regime is also root-n but has a different unavoidable constant or non-Gaussian local experiment, say and prove that rather than inventing a slower exponent. Update the separated proposition so its uniform gap is part of the stratum definition or a separately named subclass, not an ad hoc premise appended only to obtain its conclusion.

Retain angle 0, but perform one root-level redesign rather than another local patch. Make the headline upper-construction claim an explicit CONJECTURE, not a theorem: rename thm:uniform-honesty-upper-length to con:uniform-honesty-upper-length, set kind="conjecture", preserve status="to-prove", and propagate that status through the two-regime statement, TLDR, results-at-a-glance, project justification, dependency graph, and prose. Do not encode honesty, expected length, testing-modulus behavior, or frontier attainment as assumptions, and do not introduce any ad hoc slow rate.

Eliminate the growing Bonferroni family at its source. For each declared stratum j, define a single deterministic smoothing bandwidth h^\circ_{j,n} as the largest minimizer over the finite grid H_{j,n} of h^{alpha_j+1}+rho_{j,n}^2/h. Evaluate the score, estimator, studentizer, bias envelope, and Lepski comparison only at the J candidates (j,h^\circ_{j,n}), rather than at every bandwidth in the growing union of grids. Define the fixed critical value z^\star_{J,gamma}=z_{1-gamma/(4J)} and candidate half-width w_j=z^\star_{J,gamma}\widehat sigma_{j,h^\circ_{j,n}}/sqrt(n)+b_j(h^\circ_{j,n},n). Rewrite the measurable selection rule and C_n^\star using these J candidates, with explicit deterministic tie breaking. The relevant simultaneous event is then a finite-J Bonferroni event and z^\star_{J,gamma} is independent of n. State the conjectured upper length as D_{j,gamma}[n^{-1/2}+r_{j,n}], where r_{j,n}=min_{h in H_{j,n}}{h^{alpha_j+1}+rho_{j,n}^2/h}; do not retain any critical value whose probability level depends on sum_l |H_{l,n}|. Explain that the bandwidth grid remains only a deterministic device for defining h^\circ_{j,n}, not a family of simultaneously studentized candidates. Ensure all constants and sample-size conventions remain valid if a fold contains too few treated observations, by giving the learner a deterministic measurable fallback.

Split ass:admissible-index genuinely by geometry. For nonseparated indices delta=0, retain the witness-driven restriction 0<alpha<=1/s together with C kappa^alpha>=1. For separated indices delta>0, require p=0 and C delta^alpha>=1 but allow arbitrary alpha>0; the power-profile Hölder restriction is irrelevant because the exhibited member has constant contrast. Give explicit nonvacuous examples in the sanity section: for d=1, s=2, alpha=1, any feasible fixed delta>0 and C>=delta^{-1} satisfy 2/(4+1)>3/8, so r_{j,n}=o(n^{-1/2}) and the conjectured length is O(n^{-1/2}); separately give a feasible nonseparated example such as d=1, s=1, alpha=1/2, delta=0, p=1/2, with C chosen to satisfy C kappa^{1/2}>=1. Recheck kappa, outcome-bound, variance-floor, Hölder-radius, margin, and grid-nonemptiness conditions for both examples. Do not claim that the separated root-n condition holds under the nonseparated alpha<=1/s branch.

Introduce a distinct integer wavelet resolution q_{j,n}=max{q in Z_{\ge0}:2^q<=(n/log n)^{1/(2s_j+d)}} for sufficiently large n, with an explicit q_{j,n}=0 fallback when the set is empty. Replace every occurrence of wavelet resolution 2^{r_{j,n}} by 2^{q_{j,n}}. Keep r_{j,n} exclusively as the real-valued upper-length rate. Update the learner definition, symbol table, lemma dependencies, approximation/stochastic-rate explanation, and bias-envelope justification so the wavelet rate rho_{j,n} follows from this rounded integer level; absorb only genuine fixed rounding constants into D_j and b_j.

Add WhitehouseAusternSyrgkanis2025 to the bibliography using the verified 2025 arXiv:2507.11780 metadata, the related-work discussion, the comparator-promise table, and the headline conjecture’s novelty/gap text. State precisely that Whitehouse, Austern, and Syrgkanis provide smoothing-based optimal-policy-value inference and claim root-n convergence without parametric outcome-regression restrictions or strong margin assumptions, whereas the present proposed kernel concerns one connected interval honest over a predeclared finite union and its stratumwise expected length/testing-modulus adaptation problem. Do not claim priority for smoothing or root-n optimal-value inference. Differentiate Wu--Wang, Xu--Guo, Whitehouse--Austern--Syrgkanis, Luedtke--van der Laan, and Cai--Low individually in the comparator table.

Retain the testing-modulus lower bound as a theorem only if its two-point argument is independently justified. Recast the two-regime reduction as a conditional proposition/corollary of the headline conjecture, or as a conjecture itself, so it does not present an unproved consequence as established. Preserve the adaptive-frontier OEQ as the residual question: whether the conjectured construction is rate-sharp and which ordered pairs force an adaptation penalty. Audit all statement labels, used_by fields, depends_on fields, results-at-a-glance entries, the 13-section structure, bibliography citations, comparator promises, estimand_functional, technical limitation, and honest-scope language for consistency with this redesign.

ROOT DIRECTIVE FOR v5: Perform one substantive mathematical repair, not a relabeling. Delete the degenerate pointwise object \(\mathfrak L^{\mathrm{asy}}_{j,n}\) everywhere. For every deterministic positive benchmark sequence \(a=(a_n)\), define the sequence-level asymptotic minimax coefficient
\[
\mathfrak R^{\mathrm{asy}}_j(a)
=
\inf_{(C_n)\in\mathfrak C^{\mathrm{asy}}(\mathcal U,\gamma)}
\limsup_{n\to\infty}
\frac{\sup_{P\in\mathcal P_j}E_{P^n}[\operatorname{length}(C_n)]}{a_n}.
\]
Keep asymptotic honesty as a property of the entire interval sequence; do not take a separate infimum at each \(n\), and do not introduce a finite-\(n\) honest class unless the proposed construction is actually proved finite-\(n\) honest.

Restate the testing theorem consistently at sequence level. For every asymptotically honest \((C_n)\) and every \(P_n\in\mathcal P_j,\ Q_n\in\mathcal U\) satisfying \(0<|V(P_n)-V(Q_n)|\) and \(H^2(P_n,Q_n)\le1/(8n)\), prove by transferring the \(Q_n\)-coverage event to \(P_n\) that
\[
\liminf_n
\frac{E_{P_n^n}[\operatorname{length}(C_n)]}
{|V(P_n)-V(Q_n)|}
\ge \tfrac12-2\gamma.
\]
Then, using near-maximizers of the ordered modulus and explicitly handling attainment and zero-denominator issues, derive
\[
\liminf_n
\frac{\sup_{P\in\mathcal P_j}E_{P^n}[\operatorname{length}(C_n)]}
{\omega^{\mathrm{test}}_{j,n}}
\ge \tfrac12-2\gamma
\]
whenever \(\omega^{\mathrm{test}}_{j,n}>0\) eventually, and hence
\(\mathfrak R^{\mathrm{asy}}_j((\omega^{\mathrm{test}}_{j,n})_n)\ge\tfrac12-2\gamma\). Prove eventual positivity or state the consequence only under that explicit condition. Express the conjectural upper result as finiteness of
\(\mathfrak R^{\mathrm{asy}}_j((n^{-1/2}+r_{j,n})_n)\), while retaining the displayed coverage and expected-length assertion for the particular sequence \(C_n^\star\). Reformulate the adaptive-frontier OEQ as comparison of admissible benchmark sequences/rates under \(\mathfrak R^{\mathrm{asy}}_j\); do not claim that a pointwise expected-length frontier exists.

Repair nonseparated feasibility at the Hölder boundary. State the exact \(C^s\) convention and replace \(\alpha_j\le1/s_j\) by
\[
1/\alpha_j\ge s_j\quad\text{if }s_j\notin\mathbb N,
\qquad
1/\alpha_j>s_j\quad\text{if }s_j\in\mathbb N,
\]
equivalently retaining equality only at noninteger smoothness. With \(\beta_j=1/\alpha_j\), verify directly that
\(f_j(x)=(x_1-\bar p_j)_+^{\beta_j}\in C^{s_j}(\mathcal X)\), that its tie mass is \(\bar p_j\), and that its sublevel-set calculation yields the declared margin inequality with the stated constants. Only after this verification may \(\|f_j\|_{C^{s_j}}\), \(\kappa_j\), nonemptiness, and the sanity examples be asserted. Update the admissible parameter set and every dependent assumption, lemma, example, justification, and prose passage.

Add a source-verified Shi–Lu–Song (2020) bibliography entry, related-work discussion, and comparator-promise-table row. Describe its nonregular optimal-value confidence intervals and average-length comparison accurately, then distinguish them from the present unresolved target: one connected interval uniformly asymptotically honest over a predeclared finite union and a sequence-level ordered-modulus adaptation criterion. Do not present that distinction as novelty already proved.

Preserve the roles: the interval honesty/upper-length assertion remains an OEQ/conjecture; the corrected two-point testing result remains the theorem/lower bound; the exact adaptation diagram remains an OEQ. Synchronize the symbol table, definitions, statements, dependencies, gaps, consumers, TLDR, project justification, related work, technical limitation, honest scope, and comparator table so no occurrence of the old pointwise \(\mathfrak L^{\mathrm{asy}}_{j,n}\), the invalid integer-boundary witness, or the incomplete comparator account survives.

FINAL v6 ROOT LITERATURE/POSITIONING AUDIT. Preserve every current mathematical definition, statement role, field-tier scope caveat, and zero-soundness repair. Add the source-verified Armstrong and Kolesar (2020), Simple and Honest Confidence Intervals in Nonparametric Regression, Quantitative Economics 11, 1--39, DOI 10.3982/QE1199, to the bibliography, literature checklist, related work, comparator-promise table, and the uniform-honesty/upper-length conjecture gap text. State accurately that Armstrong--Kolesar construct bias-aware honest confidence intervals under predeclared smoothness restrictions and emphasize the obstruction to honest data-driven smoothness adaptation; do not claim that their result covers a causal max functional, tie atoms, or the present finite union.

Make the distinction explicit and complete: here J, every stratum tuple (smoothness/radius, margin exponent/constant, tie-mass/separation parameters), the deterministic bandwidth grids/candidate rule, outcome/design bounds, randomization, and gamma are fixed before sampling; the proposed interval uses the data only for cross-fitted nuisance fits, score/variance/bias-envelope evaluation, and the measurable candidate/interval selection already defined. Uniform asymptotic honesty is required over the entire finite predeclared union, not over an unrestricted continuum or an unknown smoothness class. Explain that this does not evade the Armstrong--Kolesar warning: any claimed adaptation is only across the declared union and remains conjectural, while the sequence-level testing-modulus theorem measures the unavoidable penalty.

Run one exhaustive comparator synchronization rather than another one-paper patch. Individually distinguish Armstrong--Kolesar (bias-aware honest CIs and smoothness-adaptation limits), Cai--Low (ordered moduli for linear Gaussian functionals), Luedtke--van der Laan (exceptional-law/pathwise differentiability), Shi--Lu--Song (subagging coverage and average-length comparisons), Wu--Wang (smoothed robust/resampling inference), Xu--Guo (adaptive smoothing and estimator-class variance bound), and Whitehouse--Austern--Syrgkanis (softmax optimal-value inference). Say exactly what each supplies and what none establishes: one connected interval uniformly asymptotically honest over this predeclared causal tie/margin/smoothness union plus its sequence-level stratumwise expected-length/testing-modulus adaptation diagram. Do not claim priority for smoothing, root-n inference, generic ordered-modulus theory, or unknown-smoothness adaptation.

Synchronize bibliography keys/citations, source checklist, related_work, comparator_promise_table, TLDR, project_justification gap/niche/fill, conjecture and OEQ gap text, honest_scope, technical limitation, and results-at-a-glance so the headline remains a conditional field-tier program: the testing lower bound and tie-plateau construction are settled targets, while uniform upper construction and exact adaptation diagram remain conjectural/open. Do not strengthen any claim or assumption.

Continue angle 1, but rebuild the proposal wholesale around a sharp causal adaptation frontier rather than finite-union uniformization itself. Preserve the binary optimal-value, tie/margin/smoothness-union setting, but demote thm:testing-modulus-lower-bound to explicitly cited Cai--Low supporting machinery: state that the two-point expected-length argument is standard, remove every novelty claim for its causal/Hellinger repackaging, and rewrite its gap, justification, consumer, TLDR, novelty justification, related work, honest scope, and comparator table accordingly. Make the focal subfield-level solve target an explicit causal least-favorable calculation for at least one fully specified ordered pair of strata: construct valid cross-stratum laws, calculate their product-testing distance and optimal-value separation, and determine whether the resulting ordered modulus is strictly larger than the oracle stratumwise rate. A generic definition of an ordered modulus, a same-stratum root-n tie perturbation, or an unanswered finite-union question is not sufficient. Target a matched achievable-versus-impossible boundary up to constants or stated logarithms; if attainment remains open, label it and every dependent regime/frontier statement as an open-ended question and do not write as though a matched frontier has been proved. Structure the declared indices or ordered pairs as needed for a nonzero adaptation penalty, since an arbitrary finite collection of strata need not exhibit one.

Audit the proposed upper interval before retaining it. The present Lepski rule compares a candidate valid on the true stratum with candidates whose nuisance and smoothing bias bounds are only stated on their own strata, so membership of the true candidate in the acceptance set and union honesty do not presently follow. Supply cross-stratum candidate bounds, nesting/ordering assumptions, or a revised union-valid selection and bias calibration; otherwise remove this construction as a promised upper solution and keep it only as an explicitly unresolved candidate. Re-derive the separated and nonseparated rates from the actual smooth-max approximation, nuisance remainder, and selection rule, and synchronize o(n^{-1/2}) conditions and all downstream OEQs.

Remove the unsupported global B/2 interior-means assumption and its Audibert--Tsybakov attribution. From |Y(a)|≤B and Var(Y(a)|X)≥v_0, derive the honest envelope |E[Y(a)|X]|≤sqrt(B^2-v_0); use that derived envelope consistently in the nuisance space, learner clipping, constants, feasible members, and score bounds. For the tie-plateau submodel, impose only the explicit local slack and t-range needed to preserve the outcome support, variance floor, Hölder radius, tie band, and margin condition. If any stronger interior restriction is retained, mark it as a novel modeling restriction, justify its scientific scope, and do not call it standard.

Strengthen the causal contribution through a source-located comparison with Whitehouse--Chen--Austern--Syrgkanis, Shi--Lu--Song, and the other optimal-value procedures: identify a precise uniform-honesty guarantee, wider tie/margin/nuisance class, or frontier statement that those sources do not establish, without claiming an improvement until it is actually derived. Retain the same-stratum tie-plateau lemma only as supporting causal geometry and state clearly that its root-n component alone is not an adaptation lower bound. Recheck every definition, theorem/OEQ status, dependency, assumption tag, gap, consumer, results-at-a-glance entry, introduction claim, project justification, limitation, bibliography citation, and comparator promise so that the packet consistently distinguishes standard machinery, proved or to-prove lemmas, and genuinely open upper/frontier results. The revised packet must honestly target a subfield-level causal adaptation theorem; if no explicit ordered pair yields a strict and plausibly matchable causal penalty, say so and recommend a later pivot rather than laundering the generic finite-union question as novelty.

CONTINUE. Preserve thm:sparse-near-tie-adaptation-lower-bound exactly as a theorem under the explicit regime 3s_1 alpha<d, where xi<1/2 and the sparse-mixture separation gives a strictly slower-than-root-n union-honesty lower bound. In def:admissible-pair, remove 3s_1 alpha<d from general construction admissibility while retaining s_1 alpha<1, 0<s_1<s_0, and beta in (s_1,1/alpha); impose 3s_1 alpha<d explicitly in the lower theorem and every claim invoking its strict penalty. Replace oeq:frontier-boundary by: “Since xi-1/2=(3s_1 alpha-d)/[2(d+s_1 alpha+4s_1)], the displayed sparse family has delta_n asymptotic to n^{-xi}, equal to root-n order when 3s_1 alpha=d and o(n^{-1/2}) when 3s_1 alpha>d. Is a root-n union-honest connected interval attainable when 3s_1 alpha>=d, or does a different least-favorable family establish a binding obstruction there? When 3s_1 alpha<d, can a union-honest interval attain the proved n^{-xi} lower scale?” Keep this statement open-ended and retain dependencies on def:admissible-pair, def:sequence-frontier, thm:sparse-near-tie-adaptation-lower-bound, and oeq:matching-union-interval. Synchronize the OEQ justification, gap, consumer, TLDR, project justification, honest scope, technical limitation, novelty justification, results-at-a-glance entry, introduction/summary prose, and any occurrence of “phase boundary,” “penalty,” or “admissible pair” so that: below the boundary the existing theorem proves the strict n^{-xi} lower penalty; at equality this family yields root-n order; above it the family yields only a nonbinding o(n^{-1/2}) lower bound; and attainability or any stronger obstruction for 3s_1 alpha>=d remains open. Do not describe the matching upper route, root-n attainability, or global sharpness of 3s_1 alpha=d as a theorem, and introduce no new novelty or optimality claims.

Revise the proto-core wholesale while preserving its field-tier status, the exact sparse-mixture lower theorem, the regime restriction 3s_1 alpha<d, and every accepted scope caveat. Make no new upper-bound, attainment, sharp-frontier, global-adaptation-diagram, or priority claim. Apply all of the following synchronized changes and leave all unrelated mathematical content unchanged.

1. Repair lem:mean-envelope at the identification root. Replace its statement so that under ass:consistency, ass:exchangeability, ass:randomization, ass:bounded-outcomes, and ass:variance-floor, consistency, conditional exchangeability, and positivity from known randomization identify m_{P,a}(x)=E_F[Y(a)|X=x] for P_X-almost every x, and consequently |m_{P,a}(x)|<=bar B=sqrt(B^2-v_0). Replace depends_on by exactly those five assumptions. State in justification that identification precedes the conditional second-moment calculation. Call both facts standard supporting facts, not contributions. Retain the consumer. Add lem:mean-envelope to used_by for consistency, exchangeability, randomization, bounded outcomes, and variance floor without deleting existing uses.

2. Add bibliography entries: PonomarevSemenova2024 = K. Ponomarev and V. Semenova (2024), Inference on optimal welfare, arXiv:2410.07443, revised September 2025; LiuLiuSasakiWan2025 = R. Liu, Z. Liu, Y. Sasaki, and Y. Wan (2025), Nonparametric classification and policy value inference, arXiv:2511.14700. Use only reviewer-verified claims: Ponomarev--Semenova study uniformly valid lower confidence bands for optimal welfare under non-uniqueness via moment inequalities and separation/margin considerations; Liu--Liu--Sasaki--Wan establish Gaussian optimal-policy-value inference using a strictly convex surrogate under distinct representative-policy assumptions.

3. Add comparator-promise entries. For PonomarevSemenova2024 state that it constructs uniformly valid one-sided lower confidence bands robust to non-unique policies through moment-inequality tests, but not the present two-sided connected interval, union-honest expected-length lower bound, or sparse-mixture construction. For LiuLiuSasakiWan2025 state that it proves Gaussian optimal-policy-value inference using a strictly convex surrogate under distinct representative-policy assumptions, but not the exact-tie ordered union or its two-sided union-honest expected-length lower bound. Set matched_by=unmatched and match_kind=downgraded_to_informed_by.

4. Replace related_work wholesale so it individually distinguishes Luedtke--van der Laan, Wu--Wang, Shi--Lu--Song, Xu--Guo, Whitehouse--Chen--Austern--Syrgkanis, Ponomarev--Semenova, Liu--Liu--Sasaki--Wan, Cai--Low, Armstrong--Kolesar, Audibert--Tsybakov, and Liu--Zhang--Nie--Soon. Explicitly say Ponomarev--Semenova provide one-sided non-uniqueness-robust lower bands, and Liu--Liu--Sasaki--Wan give Gaussian inference under distinct representative-policy assumptions; neither establishes a connected interval uniformly asymptotically honest over the displayed ordered union or the sparse-mixture expected-length penalty. Keep Cai--Low as standard reduction and Armstrong--Kolesar as bias-protection context.

5. Replace the gap of thm:sparse-near-tie-adaptation-lower-bound to list both new comparators and state that none of all comparators gives the displayed two-sided union-honest expected-length lower bound from the explicit dominated causal sparse randomized-sign mixture. The claimed contribution remains only the full-data membership audit, product chi-square calculation, and strict cross-stratum penalty for the ordered pair when 3s_1 alpha<d. Do not alter the theorem statement, rate, constants, dependencies, justification, consumer, or status except citation synchronization.

6. Replace the gap of oeq:matching-union-interval to distinguish Armstrong--Kolesar, Ponomarev--Semenova one-sided bands, and Liu--Liu--Sasaki--Wan Gaussian inference, and state none proves a two-sided connected interval uniformly honest over this exact-tie/near-tie union with the requested expected-length control. Retain it as an OEQ and do not claim def:union-upper-route works.

7. Synchronize project prose. project_justification.gap must say existing work provides smoothing, subagging, exceptional-law methods, one-sided bands robust to non-uniqueness, or Gaussian inference under distinct representative-policy assumptions, but not the displayed sparse causal mixture and its two-sided union-honest expected-length cost. Retain niche and fill, with the upper route only an open handle. novelty_justification.published_axis must distinguish both new comparators and limit the proposed advance to the mixture membership audit, product chi-square calculation, and 3s_1 alpha<d strict-penalty regime. Explicitly disclaim a matching upper interval, globally sharp threshold, complete frontier, and priority for the adjacent methods. Add both comparator distinctions to the TLDR while retaining that the matching interval and stronger obstruction are open. Do not strengthen technical_internal_limitation or honest_scope.

8. Add literature-checklist entries for both papers, with relevant_to pointing to thm:sparse-near-tie-adaptation-lower-bound and oeq:matching-union-interval and the same one-sided/distinct-assumption distinctions.

9. Final global synchronization audit: every citation has a bibliography entry; both new keys occur in related_work, comparator_promise_table, and literature_checklist; every novelty description distinguishes the focal theorem from both papers; every dependency representation of lem:mean-envelope includes consistency, exchangeability, randomization/positivity, bounded outcomes, and variance floor. Preserve the sparse family and focal lower theorem exactly in scope and both accepted caveats. Make no new upper or frontier claim anywhere.
